data.text: I found the flagged package under `cli/internal/escalation`, and it is already referenced by server/loop code plus tests. Next I’m checking whether those references are actually on the production path or only in test-only / dead branches so I can make a clean wire-or-delete call.

The functions are not standalone utilities; they are used by the worker loop and loop escalation code. I’m now tracing whether those callers are actually reachable from `main()` in the production binary, because the deadcode report can still flag a package that is only exercised by tests or behind a dead import chain.

data.text: I have enough context to check the root cause mechanically. Next I’m verifying the module layout and current worktree state, then I’ll either make a minimal wire fix or conclude this is a false positive / already-wired case and write the required decisions log.

1	package main
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"fmt"
     5		"os"
     6	
     7		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/cmd"
     8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
     9	)
    10	
    11	var version = "dev"
    12	var commit = "unknown"
    13	var date = "unknown"
    14	
    15	func main() {
    16		// Set version info for cobra
    17		cmd.Version = version
    18		cmd.Commit = commit
    19		cmd.Date = date
    20	
    21		// Get working directory once at startup, then resolve to the git
    22		// repository root so that ddx always operates from the project root
    23		// regardless of which subdirectory the user invokes it from.
    24		cwd, err := os.Getwd()
    25		if err != nil {
    26			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error getting working directory: %v\n", err)
    27			os.Exit(1)
    28		}
    29		workingDir := git.FindProjectRoot(cwd)
    30	
    31		if err := cmd.Execute(workingDir); err != nil {
    32			// Check if it's an ExitError with a specific exit code
    33			if exitErr, ok := err.(*cmd.ExitError); ok {
    34				// Print error message only if it's not empty
    35				if exitErr.Message != "" {
    36					fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error: %v\n", exitErr.Message)
    37				}
    38				os.Exit(exitErr.Code)
    39			}
    40			// For other errors, print them

1	package escalation
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"fmt"
     5		"strings"
     6		"sync"
     7	)
     8	
     9	// InfrastructureFailurePatterns are detail substrings that indicate a
    10	// transient infrastructure-level failure (provider 502, network unreachable,
    11	// command not found, auth/quota exhausted) rather than a model-capability
    12	// failure (test failed, build failed, no changes after attempt).
    13	//
    14	// Infrastructure failures should not consume escalation budget — the bead
    15	// should be deferred with a retry-after, not retried at the next tier with
    16	// a more expensive model. The model wasn't given a fair chance.
    17	//
    18	// Patterns are matched case-insensitively as substrings of the failure
    19	// detail string. Any new pattern added here is automatically picked up by
    20	// IsInfrastructureFailure callers.
    21	var InfrastructureFailurePatterns = []string{
    22		// HTTP-level provider unavailability
    23		"502", "503", "504",
    24		"bad gateway",
    25		"service unavailable",
    26		"gateway timeout",
    27		// Network-level unreachability
    28		"connection refused",
    29		"no such host",
    30		"no route to host",
    31		"network is unreachable",
    32		"i/o timeout",
    33		"context deadline exceeded",
    34		// Auth/quota exhaustion (operator-fixable, not a model fault)
    35		"401", "429",
    36		"unauthorized",
    37		"rate limit",
    38		"ratelimit",
    39		"quota exceeded",
    40		"insufficient quota",
    41		"insufficient_quota",
    42		// Subprocess harness binary missing or unstartable
    43		"command not found",
    44		"executable file not found",
    45		"no such file or directory",
    46	}
    47	
    48	// IsInfrastructureFailure reports whether the given failure status + detail
    49	// indicates a transient infrastructure problem the model could not have
    50	// fixed. Only execution_failed can be infrastructure; other escalatable
    51	// statuses are semantic outcomes that should proceed through the tier ladder.
    52	// Returns false for statuses whose detail does not match any known
    53	// infrastructure pattern.
    54	//
    55	// Used by the execute-loop to decide whether to (a) defer the bead with a
    56	// retry-after and try the same tier later, or (b) burn through to the next
    57	// tier as the standard escalation policy.
    58	func IsInfrastructureFailure(status, detail string) bool {
    59		if status != "execution_failed" {
    60			return false
    61		}
    62		if detail == "" {
    63			return false
    64		}
    65		lower := strings.ToLower(detail)
    66		for _, p := range InfrastructureFailurePatterns {
    67			if strings.Contains(lower, p) {
    68				return true
    69			}
    70		}
    71		return false
    72	}
    73	
    74	// DefaultMaxCostUSD is the per-execute-loop dollar cap on accumulated billed
    75	// spend. Exceeding the cap halts further bead claiming so an execute-loop
    76	// run cannot silently burn through arbitrary openrouter / pay-per-token
    77	// credits. Local LLMs and subscription-bundled providers do not count
    78	// toward the cap (see CountsTowardCostCap).
    79	//
    80	// $100 is large enough not to interrupt normal subscription-driven work
    81	// (where billed cost stays at $0) and small enough to surface a clear stop
    82	// signal before runaway openrouter usage.
    83	const DefaultMaxCostUSD = 100.0
    84	
    85	// CostClassBilled is the HarnessInfo.CostClass value the agent uses for
    86	// pay-per-token providers. Free local providers report "free" and
    87	// subscription-bundled providers report "subscription".
    88	const CostClassBilled = "expensive"
    89	
    90	// CountsTowardCostCap returns true when an attempt's reported CostUSD
    91	// should accumulate against the loop's MaxCostUSD. Callers pass the
    92	// HarnessInfo of the harness that ran the attempt; when info is nil the
    93	// safe default is to count (treat unknown as billed).
    94	func CountsTowardCostCap(isLocal, isSubscription bool, costClass string) bool {
    95		if isLocal {
    96			return false
    97		}
    98		if isSubscription {
    99			return false
   100		}
   101		// CostClass "free" or "subscription" never bill; only "expensive" does.
   102		if costClass != "" && costClass != CostClassBilled {
   103			return false
   104		}
   105		return true
   106	}
   107	
   108	// HarnessBilledLookup reports whether a harness's reported CostUSD should
   109	// be accumulated against a CostCapTracker. Implementations typically
   110	// consult a service.ListHarnesses snapshot. A nil lookup is treated as
   111	// "count by default" (the safe option for unknown harnesses).
   112	type HarnessBilledLookup func(harnessName string) bool
   113	
   114	// CostCapTracker accumulates billed cost for a single execute-loop run
   115	// and reports when accumulated spend has reached the configured cap.
   116	// Safe for concurrent use across goroutines (workers may call Add and
   117	// Tripped concurrently).
   118	//
   119	// MaxUSD == 0 disables the cap; Tripped always returns (_, false). The
   120	// Lookup callback decides which harnesses contribute to the running
   121	// total — local and subscription-bundled providers should be excluded
   122	// (see CountsTowardCostCap).
   123	type CostCapTracker struct {
   124		MaxUSD float64
   125		Lookup HarnessBilledLookup
   126	
   127		mu    sync.Mutex
   128		spent float64
   129		// cache memoizes Lookup results so we do not rebuild a service for
   130		// every reported attempt. Protected by mu.
   131		cache map[string]bool
   132	}
   133	
   134	// NewCostCapTracker constructs a tracker with the given dollar cap and
   135	// lookup. Pass maxUSD <= 0 to disable the cap. A nil lookup is treated
   136	// as "count by default" — every harness contributes.
   137	func NewCostCapTracker(maxUSD float64, lookup HarnessBilledLookup) *CostCapTracker {
   138		return &CostCapTracker{
   139			MaxUSD: maxUSD,
   140			Lookup: lookup,
   141			cache:  map[string]bool{},
   142		}
   143	}
   144	
   145	// counts reports whether harnessName's CostUSD should accumulate. The
   146	// result is memoized; callers that want fresh resolution should
   147	// construct a new tracker. Lookups for empty harness names default to
   148	// true (count) so accidentally-missing harness metadata never silently
   149	// bypasses the cap.
   150	func (t *CostCapTracker) counts(harnessName string) bool {
   151		if harnessName == "" {
   152			return true
   153		}
   154		t.mu.Lock()
   155		if v, ok := t.cache[harnessName]; ok {
   156			t.mu.Unlock()
   157			return v
   158		}
   159		t.mu.Unlock()
   160		result := true
   161		if t.Lookup != nil {
   162			result = t.Lookup(harnessName)
   163		}
   164		t.mu.Lock()
   165		t.cache[harnessName] = result
   166		t.mu.Unlock()
   167		return result
   168	}
   169	
   170	// Add accumulates costUSD against the running total when harnessName's
   171	// billing class counts toward the cap. Non-positive cost is ignored.
   172	func (t *CostCapTracker) Add(harnessName string, costUSD float64) {
   173		if costUSD <= 0 {
   174			return
   175		}
   176		if !t.counts(harnessName) {
   177			return
   178		}
   179		t.mu.Lock()
   180		t.spent += costUSD
   181		t.mu.Unlock()
   182	}
   183	
   184	// Spent returns the current accumulated billed total.
   185	func (t *CostCapTracker) Spent() float64 {
   186		t.mu.Lock()
   187		defer t.mu.Unlock()
   188		return t.spent
   189	}
   190	
   191	// Tripped reports whether the accumulated spend has reached MaxUSD. When
   192	// MaxUSD <= 0 the cap is disabled and Tripped returns (_, false).
   193	// Returns the formatted operator-facing detail string when tripped so
   194	// callers can populate a stop-the-loop ExecuteBeadReport.
   195	func (t *CostCapTracker) Tripped() (string, bool) {
   196		if t.MaxUSD <= 0 {
   197			return "", false
   198		}
   199		t.mu.Lock()
   200		spent := t.spent
   201		t.mu.Unlock()
   202		if spent < t.MaxUSD {
   203			return "", false
   204		}
   205		return fmt.Sprintf("cost cap reached: $%.2f billed >= $%.2f cap; raise the cap or set 0 to disable. Subscription and local providers do not count.", spent, t.MaxUSD), true
   206	}

1	package cmd
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"context"
     5		"fmt"
     6		"io"
     7		"os"
     8		"path/filepath"
     9		"strings"
    10		"time"
    11	
    12		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
    13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/workerprobe"
    14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
    15		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
    16		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/escalation"
    17		serverpkg "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/server"
    18		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
    19	)
    20	
    21	// Exit codes for ddx try terminal outcomes. These are specific to ddx try
    22	// and intentionally do not reuse the generic ExitCode* constants because
    23	// the bead-execution outcome vocabulary is distinct from generic CLI errors.
    24	const (
    25		tryExitSuccess         = 0 // success or already_satisfied
    26		tryExitPreservedOrNone = 1 // preserved_for_review or no_changes
    27		tryExitFailed          = 2 // execution_failed or any other terminal non-success
    28	)
    29	
    30	// newTryCommand creates the top-level "ddx try <bead-id>" command.
    31	// It targets a single specific bead through the full production execution
    32	// machinery (claim → execute → land → review), unlike "ddx work" which drains
    33	// the queue by picker priority and cannot force a specific bead.
    34	func (f *CommandFactory) newTryCommand() *cobra.Command {
    35		cmd := &cobra.Command{
    36			Use:          "try <bead-id>",
    37			Short:        "Execute a specific bead through the production machinery",
    38			SilenceUsage: true,
    39			Long: `try executes a single named bead through the full production
    40	execution machinery: claim → execute → land → review.
    41	
    42	Unlike "ddx work" which drains the queue by picker priority, "ddx try"
    43	targets a specific bead ID. This is useful for:
    44	  - Operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain
    45	  - Deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution (dispatch each child
    46	    in dep order with an explicit ID)
    47	  - Path-comparison studies that need to execute one fixture bead at a time
    48	
    49	Pre-flight checks (run before any claim is issued):
    50	  - bead not found → non-zero exit with "bead not found: <id>"
    51	  - bead not claimable (status=closed, cancelled, etc.) → non-zero exit
    52	  - bead has unmet dependencies → non-zero exit listing the blockers
    53	
    54	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
    55	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
    56	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
    57	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
    58	
    59	Flag plumbing is identical to "ddx work": --harness, --model, --provider,
    60	--profile, --effort are forwarded to the agent as opaque passthrough
    61	constraints. DDx does not validate or branch on their string values.
    62	
    63	Exit codes:
    64	  0  success or already_satisfied
    65	  1  preserved_for_review or no_changes
    66	  2  execution_failed or any other terminal non-success
    67	`,
    68			Example: `  # Execute a specific bead
    69	  ddx try ddx-abc12345
    70	
    71	  # Execute with an explicit harness and model
    72	  ddx try ddx-abc12345 --harness codex --model gpt-5.4-mini
    73	
    74	  # Execute without the post-merge review step (break-glass only)
    75	  ddx try ddx-abc12345 --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing`,
    76			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
    77			RunE: f.runTry,
    78		}
    79	
    80		// Routing knobs — identical to "ddx work" passthrough surface.
    81		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
    82		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
    83		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
    84		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
    85		cmd.Flags().String("profile", agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart")
    86		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
    87		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model catalog reference (e.g. code-medium); resolved via the model catalog")
    88		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
    89		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
    90		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
    91		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
    92		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
    93		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
    94		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained)")
    95		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained)")
    96	
    97		return cmd
    98	}
    99	
   100	// runTry is the RunE for "ddx try <bead-id>".
   101	func (f *CommandFactory) runTry(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
   102		beadID := args[0]
   103	
   104		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
   105		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
   106		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
   107		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
   108		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
   109		profile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("profile")
   110		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
   111		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
   112		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
   113		noReview, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review")
   114		noReviewAck, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing")
   115		reviewHarness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-harness")
   116		reviewModel, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-model")
   117		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
   118		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
   119		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
   120
